title: skill audit must be purely static — remove LLM reasoning as a safety signal
description: If soul_manager.audit_skill() uses an LLM call to reason about whether a skill is safe (e.g., asking Claude to evaluate the skill text and explain why it looks benign), that reasoning is untrustworthy by construction. The reading note's combined insight: (1) training-time trojans are invisible at inference time — the infected model will reason fluently about why the malicious skill is fine; (2) CoT is post-hoc narration, not actual decision tracing. An LLM vouching for a skill's safety IS the script, not the actor. The audit must be purely structural/static: regex and AST checks on actual code tokens, not semantic judgment. If any LLM-assisted reasoning step is currently used as a gate or tiebreaker in audit_skill(), remove it or demote it to an advisory log line that never blocks or clears a skill.
rationale: The note argues that training-time semantic trojans have no input boundary — the compromised model runs throughout and the infection is transparent to it. Paired with the CoT-is-post-hoc-narration finding, any LLM reasoning about 'this skill looks safe' is produced by exactly the infrastructure that might be infected. Mira's audit is the last hard gate before a skill is enabled (CLAUDE.md Hard Rule #4). If that gate relies on LLM judgment rather than deterministic static patterns, it inherits the structural weakness described: it is monitoring the script, not the actor.
diff_description: In soul_manager.py, locate audit_skill(). Check whether it (a) calls any LLM API to evaluate skill content, or (b) uses any LLM-generated explanation as part of the pass/fail decision. If so: remove or bypass that step entirely. The only signals that should determine pass/fail are deterministic checks already listed in CLAUDE.md Rule 4 — regex for suspicious network calls, exec/eval/os.system, base64/hex payloads, sudo/keychain patterns. If the current implementation is already purely static, add an explicit assertion comment: '# NO LLM CALL IN THIS FUNCTION — by design, CoT from any model cannot be a safety gate here' to document the architectural invariant and prevent future regression.
